patch.value: SDK author-time version-compatibility preflight: checkExtensionManifestCompatibility + assertExtensionManifestCompatible
Author-time SDK primitive for the epic version-compat leg.

Deliver the epic's explicitly-listed 'version-compat' leg as an author-facing SDK primitive. pm_min_version/pm_max_version compatibility is enforced ONLY at runtime by the loader: the logic is async (reads the installed CLI package.json) and private to loader.ts, so a package author cannot statically verify their manifest version bounds against a target pm version without spinning up a real repo at a real CLI version. Add checkExtensionManifestCompatibility(manifest, target): a pure synchronous author-time analyzer returning structured per-bound findings that mirror the loader min/max semantics by construction. Add assertExtensionManifestCompatible(manifest, target): the throwing CI/test guard. Backed by a shared core/extensions/version-compat.ts the loader delegates to, so author-time and runtime checks can never drift (same 'agrees with loader by construction' philosophy as describeExtensionBlueprint/deriveExtensionCapabilities).

Delivered the epic's version-compat leg as an author-facing SDK primitive. Three children shipped: shared core/extensions/version-compat.ts (pm-sjea), checkExtensionManifestCompatibility analyzer (pm-knma), assertExtensionManifestCompatible test guard (pm-hng2). Author-time and runtime version gating now share one implementation and agree by construction. Verified end-to-end against the built dist (SDK helpers + real loader gating of pm_min/pm_max bounds). 4949 tests, 100% cov, static+docs gates green.
